chunk_text: Students must activate their Student Portal account using the credentials provided by the university to access enrollment services.
Students who forget their password can use the password recovery option in the Student Portal to reset their account credentials.
Students experiencing login issues should verify their credentials or use the password reset feature before contacting support.
Students can view their enrolled courses through the Student Portal after completing the enrollment process.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: Students must log in to the AIMS Student Portal to begin the enrollment process.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: After logging in, students should access the Online Registration System (ORS) to proceed with enrollment.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: Students must select their desired courses in the ORS before confirming enrollment.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: Students must pay tuition and other fees using approved payment methods listed in the portal.
Students are required to upload proof of payment in the AIMS portal for validation.
After submitting requirements, students must wait for enrollment confirmation from the system.
Enrollment deadlines are provided in the academic calendar and must be strictly followed.
Students must complete all required steps including course selection and payment before enrollment is finalized.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: The enrollment process includes logging in, accessing ORS, selecting courses, paying fees, uploading proof, and waiting for confirmation.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: Students must follow the approved payment methods provided by the university when settling enrollment fees.
source_title: Enrolling Courses at UP Open University

chunk_text: Follow the instructions in the portal carefully to ensure successful payment processing.
Payment validation may include OTP verification and manual confirmation.
Payments must be completed before the deadline to secure enrollment.
Students will receive confirmation once payment has been successfully validated.
Only matriculated students are officially enrolled and eligible to attend classes and access academic services.
Students must complete course selection, settle fees, and comply with all enrollment requirements to be matriculated.
The enrollment period is the official timeframe when students can register for courses and complete enrollment requirements.
Late registration may be allowed within a limited period but may be subject to additional rules or restrictions.
The add/drop period allows students to modify their enrolled courses within a specified timeframe.
Students may add courses during the allowed period through the Online Registration System.
source_title: Availability of Online Applications for Dropping, Leave of Absence, and Completion of EXT/Removal of 4.0 in the Student Portal

chunk_text: Students may drop courses within the allowed period using the Online Registration System.
source_title: Availability of Online Applications for Dropping, Leave of Absence, and Completion of EXT/Removal of 4.0 in the Student Portal

chunk_text: Withdrawal from courses must follow official procedures and may have academic or administrative implications.
source_title: Availability of Online Applications for Dropping, Leave of Absence, and Completion of EXT/Removal of 4.0 in the Student Portal

chunk_text: Students who cannot log in should verify credentials or reset their password using the portal’s recovery option.
If payment is not reflected, students should wait for processing or verify submission of proof of payment.
Enrollment may not be confirmed if required steps such as payment or validation are incomplete.
Students should check their course selection and ensure enrollment steps were properly completed if subjects are missing.
Students should retry uploading proof of payment or ensure the file meets the required format.
Students may contact the Registrar’s Office for enrollment and academic concerns.
Students may contact the Finance Office for concerns related to payment and fees.
Students may access official helpdesk links for support on enrollment and system-related concerns.

The registration period for UPOU AY 2025-2026 is as follows: 1st Semester: 11-16 August 2025 (Mon-Sat). 2nd Semester: 12-17 January 2026 (Mon-Sat). Mid-Year Term: 1-6 June 2026 (Mon-Sat). A student is officially enrolled only after completing all registration processes up to the payment of fees.
keywords: registration period, enrollment dates, 1st semester, 2nd semester, mid-year term, officially enrolled

chunk_text: Classes start on the following dates for AY 2025-2026: 1st Semester: 25 August 2025 (Mon). 2nd Semester: 26 January 2026 (Mon). Mid-Year Term: 15 June 2026 (Mon).
keywords: start of classes, first day of class, when classes begin, 1st semester, 2nd semester, mid-year

section_title: End of classes and final examination
chunk_text: Classes end and final examinations are held on: 1st Semester: 29 November 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 2 May 2026 (Sat). Mid-Year Term: 25 July 2026 (Sat).
keywords: end of classes, final exam, last day of class, final examination date

chunk_text: Mid-semester examinations for AY 2025-2026 are scheduled on: 1st Semester: 11 October 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 14 March 2026 (Sat). Mid-Year Term: 4 July 2026 (Sat).
keywords: mid-semester exam, midterm exam, midterms, examination schedule

chunk_text: UPOU uses the term Study Interlude instead of Reading Break, defined as a brief period for students to engage in self-directed study or catch up on academic tasks without the pressure of regular coursework or assessments. Study Interlude dates: 1st Semester: 30 October to 1 November 2025 (Thu-Sat). 2nd Semester: 1-3 April 2026 (Wed-Fri).
keywords: study interlude, reading break, study break, self-directed study

section_title: Deadline for filing of dropping and leave of absence
chunk_text: The deadline for filing a request to drop a subject or a leave of absence (LOA) is: 1st Semester: 15 November 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 18 April 2026 (Sat). Mid-Year Term: 18 July 2026 (Sat).
keywords: dropping, LOA, leave of absence, drop subject deadline, withdrawal

section_title: Deadline to file and take make-up and removal exams
chunk_text: The deadline for filing a request for make-up exam, removal exam, or completion of requirements is: 1st Semester: 3 November 2025 (Mon). 2nd Semester: 6 April 2026 (Mon). Mid-Year Term: 11 July 2026 (Sat). The Removal and Make-up Exams are held on: 1st Semester: 22 November 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 25 April 2026 (Sat). Mid-Year Term: 18 July 2026 (Sat).
keywords: makeup exam, removal exam, completion requirements, INC grade, incomplete

section_title: Deadline for submission of grades by faculty
chunk_text: The deadline for faculty submission of grades is: 1st Semester: 13 December 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 12 May 2026 (Tue). Mid-Year Term: 4 August 2026 (Tue). Note that the 1st Semester grade submission deadline was moved to accommodate the revised academic calendar.
keywords: submission of grades, grade deadline, when are grades due, faculty grades

section_title: Deadline for submission of application for graduation
chunk_text: The deadline for submission of application for graduation is: End of 1st Semester AY 2025-2026: 20 September 2025 (Sat). End of 2nd Semester AY 2025-2026: 21 February 2026 (Sat). End of Mid-Year Term AY 2025-2026: 27 June 2026 (Sat).
keywords: graduation application, apply for graduation, graduation deadline, candidate for graduation

section_title: Deadline for submission of graduation requirements
chunk_text: The deadline for submission of requirements for graduation is: 1st Semester: 9 December 2025 (Tue). 2nd Semester: 12 May 2026 (Tue). Mid-Year Term: 4 August 2026 (Tue).
keywords: graduation requirements, graduation clearance, final requirements

chunk_text: The UPOU 30th Commencement Exercises will be held on 5 December 2026 (Sat).
keywords: commencement, graduation ceremony, 30th commencement, graduation rites

section_title: Deadline for submission of admission applications
chunk_text: Deadline for submission of admission applications for AY 2025-2026: For programs with admission test and qualifying exam (MPM, MLLE, MIH): 1st Semester 5 April 2025 (Sat), 2nd Semester 6 September 2025 (Sat). For all other programs and transfer students: 1st Semester 19 April 2025 (Sat), 2nd Semester 20 September 2025 (Sat).

chunk_text: The deadline for submission of application for Transfer of Credits is: 1st Semester: 7 June 2025 (Sat). 2nd Semester: 8 November 2025 (Sat). chunk_text: Official holidays during the 1st Semester AY 2025-2026 include: 21 Aug 2025 Ninoy Aquino Day, 25 Aug 2025 National Heroes Day, 1 Nov 2025 All Saints Day, 2 Nov 2025 All Souls Day, 30 Nov 2025 Bonifacio Day, 8 Dec 2025 Feast of the Immaculate Conception, 24 Dec 2025 Christmas Eve, 25 Dec 2025 Christmas Day, 30 Dec 2025 Rizal Day, 31 Dec 2025 New Years Eve.

keywords: holidays, 2nd semester holidays, Holy Week, Maundy Thursday, Good Friday, Araw ng Kagitingan, Labor Day

chunk_text: The UPOU academic calendar is composed of two parts: a trimester schedule and a semester schedule. Programs in UPOU operate under the semestral system except for the undergraduate programs and the MDE and GCDE programs which follow the trimester system.
keywords: semester, trimester, academic calendar, semestral system, MDE, GCDE, undergraduate
